title: DOA spacing and bed layout for dry-zone red onion
body: For commercial dry-zone red-onion beds, the DOA calendar specifies raised beds 90 × 800 cm with a 15 × 15 cm plant spacing, giving roughly 320 plants per bed. For bulb-seed production the tighter 60 × 800 cm beds with 10 × 10 cm spacing are preferred because closer spacing produces the small 20–30 g bulbs ideal for vernalization. A third polythene cover over the bed centres protects seedlings from intense sun during the first 3 weeks after sowing.

field_provenance.source_document_title: DOA/AFACI Red Onion Cultivation Calendar (Dry Zone), 2014
